summary: Rare Cowboy jam, fiery Other One, mixed electric set
blurb: The acoustic first half shines with a rare Cowboy Song improvisation and a powerful rendition of It's A Man's World. The second half features an intense The Other One sequence but suffers from uneven sound quality on the electric material. Highlights include Not Fade Away and Turn On Your Lovelight.
review: April 9, 1970 at the Fillmore West captures one of the most intriguing snapshots of the early‑70 Dead. Pigpen’s acoustic set is a highlight – the band stretches out on an almost mythical Cowboy Song jam that many fans still talk about, and Jerry’s phrasing on It’s A Man’s World feels especially soulful. The electric half launches into a wild, double‑Cryptical Envelopment voyage into The Other One, delivering the kind of high‑octane improvisation that defined this era, before winding down with a rousing Not Fade Away/Turn On Your Lovelight finish. The night is also notable for the surprise appearance of the Miles Davis Quintet as a supporting act, adding an unexpected jazz‑inflected flavor to the evening’s energy. While the sound quality dips in places – tape warble on the acoustic side and dropouts during the electric stretch – the matrix recording (gd70-04-09.sbd.hanno.6157.sbeok.shnf) offers the clearest window into those magical moments, making it the go‑to source for anyone wanting to experience this unique show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, two are mixed; no consistent negative language. The overall sentiment leans modestly positive, warranting a small boost.

best_recording.reason: Matrix source delivers the rare Cowboy jam and clear acoustic portion, despite some electric‑set dropouts; it’s the most complete and sonically balanced representation of the show.

key_highlights: Rare Cowboy Song extended jam in the acoustic set
Fiery The Other One sequence with dual Cryptical Envelopments
Strong closing energy on Not Fade Away and Turn On Your Lovelight
Miles Davis Quintet supporting act adds historic jazz flavor
